name: Algiers Rooftop — Mirror Array
description: The peaked roof of the Algiers house, accessible from the attic window, holds a hand-assembled signal mirror array that has been 22 degrees off optimal alignment since 3:00 PM; from here the Industrial Canal corridor is visible as a dark stripe through the flooded grid to the east.
atmosphericDetails: The mirror array consists of three salvaged bathroom mirrors mounted on a wooden frame with adjustable wing-nut brackets; the frame has shifted on its base, rotating the entire assembly clockwise from optimal east-northeast alignment

From the roof peak, the St. Claude Bridge is visible as a concrete line above the flood plane approximately a mile to the east-northeast — a small dark mass of people visible on the deck in the afternoon haze
The flat white-yellow light that offers no shadow also offers no reference point for alignment; optimal positioning requires sighting against a known landmark
The roof surface is hot enough to blister unprotected skin; the tar paper is soft
